seoTitle: Use Jira with Confluence for Project Management (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to use Jira with Confluence for project management in 2026, embedding live sprint boards, creating issues from text, and building shared dashboards.
intro: Jumping between separate tabs to check sprint tasks and then copy-pasting progress into status reports wastes time and introduces errors. This guide shows how to connect Jira and Confluence in 2026 to build a single, live source of truth.

steps.heading: Open the Jira integration
steps.body: In edit mode, click your insertion point and type `/jira` to launch the integration wizard.

steps.heading: Embed an issue or query
steps.body: Paste a direct Jira issue URL or type a search query to embed a live, dynamic task card.

steps.heading: Embed a filtered list or roadmap
steps.body: Use the wizard panel to insert an entire filtered list or a high-level epic roadmap block.

steps.heading: Create issues from text
steps.body: Highlight text on the page and use the inline pop-up to spin up a new Jira issue without leaving the canvas.

steps.heading: Publish
steps.body: Click Publish to make your real-time project management dashboard live across the team.

faq.question: Will my embedded Jira card update automatically as the ticket moves through sprints?
faq.answer: Yes, real-time webhooks connect the two tools so the tracking status bar on your page updates as developers move tasks.

faq.question: Can I create a new Jira issue directly from a Confluence page?
faq.answer: Yes, highlighting a block of text and using the inline pop-up panel lets you convert it straight into a Jira issue.

faq.question: Can I embed an entire epic roadmap instead of a single issue?
faq.answer: Yes, the integration wizard supports inserting a filtered list or a high-level epic roadmap block.

faq.question: Do Jira issues automatically link back to the Confluence spec page?
faq.answer: Yes, automatic back-linking arrays help ensure Jira issues reference the exact specification page they came from.
